mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces corporate borrowing costs and lowers equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate above 5.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and falling corporate earnings, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in more than 55 percent of instances when UNRATE crosses the 5.0 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate collapsing consumer demand, which reduces S&P 500 earnings expectations and triggers negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative returns, yielding a signal Sharpe ratio below 0.1 over out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid industrial production growth exceeding 3.0 percent year-over-year signals supply-side capacity bottlenecks, prompting inflation fears and immediate equity sell-offs.
failure_condition: INDPRO spikes above 3.0 percent YoY are followed by positive 5-day returns in more than 60 percent of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real interest rates, defined as the federal funds rate exceeding the CPI inflation rate, compress equity valuation multiples and generate negative forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Positive real rates fail to predict negative 60-day returns, with out-of-sample R-squared remaining below 0.01 across all testing windows.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate household income and consumer spending, supporting corporate revenue growth and positive equity returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero predictive power for 20-day returns, with a t-statistic below 1.5 over a 10-year historical sample.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth falling below 1.0 percent indicates severe economic contraction, triggering recession risk premiums and immediate negative equity price adjustments.
failure_condition: GDP growth below 1.0 percent is followed by positive 5-day returns in more than 50 percent of observed quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence where industrial production rises while payroll employment falls indicates productivity-driven cost deflation, which boosts corporate profit margins and generates positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The INDPRO-PAYEMS divergence signal yields a negative Sharpe ratio over walk-forward testing periods, confirming no predictive edge.
